name: Farfalle with Zucchini, Yellow Squash and Mint
recipeIngredient: 12 ounces farfalle (butterfly-shaped pasta; also called bow ties)
1 tablespoon extra-virgin olive oil or unsalted butter
2 small zucchini, scrubbed, trimmed, very thinly sliced
1 small yellow squash, scrubed, trimmed, very thinly sliced
1 tablespoon grated Parmigiano-Reggiano or Pecorino, plus more for topping
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the pasta in plenty of boiling, salted water, stirring occasionally, until al dente, firm to the bite, 10 to 12 minutes.
2. Before draining, ladle out half cup of the boiling cooking water and reserve.
3. Meanwhile, heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the scallion and garlic; cook, stirring, for 2 minutes.
4. Add the zucchini and yellow squash and cook, stirring, over medium-low heat just until wilted, about 3 minutes.
5. Toss the pasta with the zucchini mixture and stir in the basil, mint, reserved cooking water and 1 tablespoon of the cheese.
6. Spoon onto plates, and serve with a light sprinkling of cheese over each serving.
Yield: 4 servings
